title: KissBleTnc::try_get_app_frame cannot distinguish no-data from parse-error
description:
File: rust/lichen-kiss/src/ble.rs
Lines: 105-129

The `try_get_app_frame` method returns `Option<AppFrame>`, which means callers cannot distinguish between:
1. No complete frame available yet (waiting for more data)
2. Frame was received but was malformed and discarded

```rust
Err(_e) => {
    #[cfg(feature = "log")]
    warn!("kiss_ble: try_get_app_frame parse error: {}", _e);
    return None;  // Looks same as "no data" case
}
```

Impact: Callers wanting to track parse errors for debugging or security monitoring cannot do so without enabling logging.

Suggestion: Consider returning `Result<Option<AppFrame>, KissError>` or provide a separate method to query last error. Low priority as the current behavior is acceptable for most use cases.
close_reason: Updated KissBleTnc::try_get_app_frame to return Result<Option<AppFrame>, KissError> instead of Option (now distinguishes parse errors from no-data case); updated documentation and example; removed unused log import. Tests pass. Related wx9m also addressed by BufferTooSmall error return.
